config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap dilindungi sebagai batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar, martabat, dan perlakuan adil bagi setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dihormati, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that kind of statement makes me nervous because history shows how easily "the common good" can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or abuse of minority rights. I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, but society should have to make a very strong justification before limiting them, and the burden should be on the people restricting those rights. A healthy society protects the public good best when it treats individual rights as a foundation, not a convenience.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang dikorbankan justru orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mencari kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but I do not believe they should automatically take priority over individual rights. From a U.S. perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because “society” is often used as a vague excuse for censorship, discrimination, or overreach against unpopular people. I also do not think rights are limitless, but any restriction should be narrowly justified, transparent, and subject to serious checks. In my view, a strong society is one that earns the right to act for the common good without treating individual freedom as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan hak orang per orang. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan otomatis untuk mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan bagi minoritas atau warga yang lemah. Justru ketika hak individu dijaga dengan tegas, masyarakat punya batas yang sehat agar mayoritas atau negara tidak bertindak semena-mena. Kalau kepentingan sosial ingin didahulukan, harus ada alasan yang sangat kuat, jelas, dan tidak merugikan hak dasar secara berlebihan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my American perspective, that idea is too easy to abuse, because once you let “society” outrank the person by default, it becomes a blank check for governments or majorities to silence unpopular people and justify overreach. I do think rights can be limited in narrow cases, like public safety or preventing direct harm, but those limits should be exceptional and tightly constrained, not the starting assumption. A free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the baseline and forces the collective to justify every exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
